originalBehavior: The published article uses a staged scrollytelling shell where the explanation panes drive the scatter, curve, boundary, and example-prediction visuals.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original step containers, sticky chart shell, and scroll observer so the compiled scene transitions still advance in order.
notes: The route still mounts from the original compiled app into an otherwise empty document body.
Only shell metadata and public-facing branding were patched around the app.
evidence: The current plan treats scrollytelling step-to-visual transitions as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage scrolls through later steps and waits for the threshold boundary and prediction example to appear.

originalBehavior: Readers see the binary scatter, logistic sigmoid, and threshold-based boundary update together as the narrative reveals each concept.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original plotted points, sigmoid curve, threshold line, classification arrows, and synchronized label updates from the compiled app.
notes: The original threshold and temperature sliders still drive the same stores that feed the chart and readouts.
The predicted class label remains tied to the same probability comparison used in the source build.
evidence: The implementation plan calls sigmoid mapping, threshold changes, and label synchronization parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage changes both the temperature and threshold sliders and waits for the prediction text to update.

originalBehavior: The published article continues past the introduction into likelihood, loss, and equation scenes that reuse the same teaching shell and chart styling.
localStatus: The local route keeps the original compiled lower sections, including the logistic-loss chart and explanatory math blocks, without extracting or rewriting them.
notes: KaTeX rendering continues through the vendored MLU math stack.
The route preserves the published section order and component layout so later scenes still line up with the scroll flow.
evidence: The plan explicitly calls out equation-display synchronization and active-scene consistency.
Shared smoke coverage waits for the loss chart to render after the route initializes.

originalBehavior: The published route included MLU branding, creator bylines, analytics, and upstream metadata or image references in the public shell.
localStatus: The local route removes those public-shell references, localizes the remaining navigation, and uses the shared footer as the only live original-page reference surface.
notes: Docs retain the upstream repo and provenance notes.
The public route keeps the teaching content intact but no longer points back to creator or project promo surfaces inside the body.
evidence: The public-surface audit blocks creator names, MLU branding, upstream domains, and analytics from shipped route code.
The route now exposes a single shared-footer reference link to the live original page.
